title: Histology Technicians
description: Prepare histological slides from tissue sections for microscopic examination and diagnosis by pathologists. May assist with research studies.

tasks.text: Cut sections of body tissues for microscopic examination, using microtomes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Embed tissue specimens into paraffin wax blocks, or infiltrate tissue specimens with wax.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Mount tissue specimens on glass slides.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Stain tissue specimens with dyes or other chemicals to make cell details visible under microscopes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate computerized laboratory equipment to dehydrate, decalcify, or microincinerate tissue samples.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Archive diagnostic material, such as histologic slides and blocks.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Maintain laboratory equipment, such as microscopes, mass spectrometers, microtomes, immunostainers, tissue processors, embedding centers, and water baths.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
